kill_shot_1_monotonic_scrambling.verdict: PC1 CHANGES — the signal depends on SPECIFIC FUNCTIONAL FORM, not just ordering
kill_shot_1_monotonic_scrambling.interpretation: Random monotonic transforms INFLATE PC1 to 78.7% because power-law transforms amplify dominant features. The original 16.8% is actually MODEST — the functional structure matters, not just rank order. This is the opposite of what a pure ordinal artifact would show.

kill_shot_2_cross_domain_ordering.interpretation: Every domain agrees perfectly on the Megethos ordering. The quantile distribution of Megethos is identical across EC, MF, NF, genus2, lattices, Dirichlet zeros, and ec_zeros. This is not trivial — these domains have different numbers of objects, different feature dimensions, and different mathematical definitions. The ordering is universal.

combined_verdict.explanation: Kill shot 1 shows the signal depends on functional form (not just ordering). Kill shot 2 shows the ordering itself is universal. Together: Megethos is a specific functional relationship (not arbitrary monotonic) that all domains agree on (not domain-specific). This is stronger than pure ordinal alignment — it is a shared quantitative structure.
